observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to create an Inventory Committee that will be responsible for the conduct of the physical inventory count, and furnish the Audit Team with the said report.
observations.managementAction: Update the Composition of the Inventor Committee
observations.reason: Composition of the Inventory Committee was not yet updated, and the municipality has no full-pledged General Services Officers.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records in accordance with Section 181 (c) of GAAM Vol. I.

Further, the Office of the Municipal Accountant should keep and maintain a complete subsidiary ledger of all the cash accounts to facilitate easy reconciliation of the Accounting and Treasury records.
observations.managementAction: Assign personnel that will handle and continue the reconciliation that had been started by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Lack of accounting personnel that will focus mainly on the reconciliation; notation of previous auditor in the cashbook of the Municipal Treasurer, one of the reason making reconciliation difficult

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and POs together with its supporting documents within five (5) working days from execution/issuance to ensure timely review and evaluation of the same.
observations.managementAction: Documents had already been submitted.
observations.reason: POs and its supporting documents were still  not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following courses of action from the Management:

The Municipal Mayor should require the members of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to render explanation or justification on the noted deficiencies.

Henceforth, procurement opportunities should be advertised in the newspaper of general circulation to ensure transparency and competition.  Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed should be posted in the PhilGEPS to promote transparency and accountability.

The contractor, CG Integra, should be required to post a warranty security in accordance with the form required under Section 62.2.3.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184. 

The Management should strictly abide by the standards set forth by the R.A. No. 9184, its Revised IRR and other relevant laws on government procurement.

We further recommended for the immediate submission of authenticated copies of all the required documents mentioned. Any disbursements should be supported with complete and proper documentation to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee together with the end-user, had already rendered their explanation/justification to the Head of the Procuring Entities (HOPE) on the deficiencies observed. This project had been advertised in the newspaper of general circulation. A warranty security had also been posted by the contractor.
observations.reason: The Municipality were not able to submit the complete documentary requirements as discussed in the AOM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to expedite the creation of the Inventory Committee that will be responsible for the conduct of the physical inventory and accomplishment of the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to be submitted to the Audit Team. Likewise, submit the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) duly inspected and certified by the GSO, and verified by the Head of the Accounting Unit for the determination of its net book value.
We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to cause the immediate submission of the requested valid proof of ownership of the Land accounts.
Moreover, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare lapsing schedule of the PPE with complete details to support the recorded depreciation expenses and the book value of the properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the expenses recognized as repairs and maintenance and ensure that there is a related asset account to which they pertain so that proper matching of expenses may be achieved.

We reiterated the prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their records in accordance with Section 181 (c) of GAAM Vol. I.  Henceforth, both offices should reconcile their records regularly, so that errors or discrepancies that may be noted are adjusted accordingly.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transmit to the Municipal Accountant the identified unrecorded disbursements and receipts. Likewise, make representations with the depository bank to obtain copies of the unrecorded debit and credit memo to facilitate recording thereof in the books of accounts. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer should ensure that the reports of disbursements and deposits and all its supporting documents be strictly monitored and transmitted to the office of the Municipal Accountant.
We also recommended the management to improve the agency’s record keeping and tracking system to ensure that every bank transaction and all other financial information be properly documented and duly recorded.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the staled checks and the Municipal Accountant to record this adjustment. Hereafter, the Municipal Treasurer should closely monitor the issuance and releases of checks and send a written notice to the payee on the existence of the check at least one month before the checks become stale.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to determine the composition of each cash account and determine the cause of the abnormal/negative balances. Likewise, immediately effect in the records the identified adjustments to correct related balances. We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all accounts are converted in accordance with the new chart of accounts provided for in COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund by speeding up the implementation of the prioritized programs, projects and activities to achieve desirable socio-economic developments and environmental outcomes.
Ensure that coordination among officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring of PAPs is being undertaken to achieve desired level of outcome.
Instruct the Municipal  	Planning Development Officer to immediately look into the status of the unimplemented developmental projects, identify causes of non-implementation and develop feasible and specific solutions to effectively implement the projects.
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to be very prudent, meticulous, conscientious and precise in evaluating the timely and perfect implementation of the project so that accomplishment of the contractor is monitored and evaluated based on required time frame of project implementation.
Should there have been delays incurred in the project implementation, liquidated damages must be imposed against the contractors concerned.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to cautiously review the required supporting documents needed for specific transactions and in certifying the completeness and propriety of supporting documents of the disbursement/payroll vouchers.
Submit the documentary requirements for the payment of CNA incentives as enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 to the Audit Team in order to verify the propriety, validity and legality of the transaction.
Ensure strict adherence to the guidelines set forth under the applicable provisions of DBM Budget Circular and General Appropriations Act on granting the CNA Incentives.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to maintain sub-accounts and separate set of ledgers for the 20% Development Fund, 5% MRRM Fund, and for each of the Municipal Economic Enterprise. Consequently, prepare separate trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of its operations, in accordance with existing regulations.
We further recommended that separate bank accounts should be opened for each of the special accounts mentioned to enable proper recording, reporting and monitoring.

We recommended that the management should strictly observe the rules and regulations regarding the purchase of real properties. Likewise, it should take the necessary actions to immediately transfer the titles of ownership of those lands that are still in the name of the previous owners to establish validity and propriety of the transactions and to avoid the risk of facing future legal issues which may be detrimental to the government.
We further recommended that the management should submit the lacking documentary requirements enumerated above to support the purchase and allow us to make a decision in audit.

We recommended the Municipality to discontinue the use of open dump site. Moreover, we urged the Municipal Mayor to immediately comply with the necessary requirements in securing an ECC needed to commence with the establishment and operation of the sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid wastes in accordance with the pertinent provisions of RA 9003.

We recommended that the Municipality establish LCPC and allocate at least 1% of its IRA for that purpose in compliance with the above provisions of RA 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 in order to ensure implementation of programs, projects and activities for the protection of children.

We recommended that the Agency to discontinue the practice of procurement resorting to reimbursements. Henceforth, all procurement must be done through competitive bidding in compliance with Section 10 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 except as provided for in Article XVI of the same act, providing for the alternative modes of procurement, subject to the prior approval of the Mayor or his duly authorized representative and whenever justified by the conditions provided therein with the proper recommendation from the Bids and Awards Committee.

We recommended that the Management observe strictly the posting of public notice of all the projects on conspicuous places in the venue of the projects at the start and end of road development projects to promote transparency and accountability and encourage public participation to secure the right of the public to information in compliance to COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Municipality prepare the LDRRMF reports in accordance with the guidelines and presentations prescribed in COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Moreover, we recommended the MDRRMO to submit the complete detailed utilization reports of LDRRMF for our reference in audit.

We recommended the Municipality to strictly observe the provisions of the Republic Act 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 in the allocation and utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.
We further recommended the concerned personnel to exercise due diligence in the preparation and accomplishment of government forms and documents required to support government transactions to ensure accuracy, completeness, validity and reliability of the same.

We recommended the Municipal Government to ensure that charges for personal services, maintenance and other operating expenses and capital outlay of the LRDDM Office are appropriately charged to the General Fund of the LGU, instead of funding the same out of the 5% LDRRM Fund.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary entry to remove the inventoriable items from the PPE. Further, we recommended the Property Officer to issue an Inventory Custodian Slip to the Accountable Officer, who are in actual custody of these items.  The Property Officer should make a separate database/lists of these items for monitoring purposes.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted accordingly at year-end

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the asset. The Management should assess the useful life of the assets to determine the related amortization or any impairment in its cost to reflect its carrying amount.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted accordingly at year-end

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to record the monthly depreciation of all the PPE of the Municipality for fair presentation in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: Recording of monthly depreciation throughout the year for 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to apply the change in accounting policy to determine the impact on the Financial Statements and consequently disclose it to the Notes to Financial Statements. We also recommended the Municipal Accountant to fill out the acquisition dates of some PPE for the determination and accuracy of accumulated depreciation as of year-end.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted accordingly at year end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:
Account for the properties acquired from CY 2007 to 2014 and record the same accordingly;
Upon completion of the mentioned PPE, transfer the same in the General Fund and compute for provision for depreciation from the time of its completion to present; and
Prospectively record the capital outlay as PPE in the Trust Fund books and transfer the same upon the completion of the project to the General Fund.

We also recommended the Municipal Engineer to conduct an inventory of the acquired assets of the Municipality based on the records of the Municipal Accountant to account the PPE and submit a status report to this Office.
observations.managementAction:
Adjusted accordingly at year end.

Conduct Inventory and Submit status report to Commission on Audit
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Engineer was not able to conduct Inventory and submit the Status Report to COA

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transmit the identified unrecorded disbursements the Municipal Accountant to facilitate recording thereof in the books of accounts.

Henceforth, strict compliance by the Municipal Treasurer with regard to the transmittal of the report of disbursements and its supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant must be enjoined and strictly monitored. The Municipal Treasurer must impose administrative disciplinary action against the Disbursing Officer/s who failed to submit the complete report of disbursement including its supporting documents.

We also recommended the Management improve the agency’s record keeping and tracking system to ensure that every bank transaction and all other financial information are properly documented and duly recorded in the books.
observations.managementAction: All disbursements had been forwarded and taken up in the books of accounts as at year end

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the GF and TF books in accordance with PPSAS 23.

The Municipal Accountant should record the farm tractor amounting to ₱2,200,000.00 as an asset while the remaining value will be recognized as a revenue in relation to paragraphs 42 and 48 of PPSAS 23, to wit:

42.	An asset acquired through a non-exchange transaction shall be initially be measured at its fair value as at the date of acquisition.

48.	Revenue from non-exchange transactions shall be measured at the amount of the increase in net assets recognized by the entity.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to fast track the processing of documents supporting the accomplishments of the projects implemented in the Trust Fund for the timely release of those funds to minimize the borrowing of funds within that fund.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted accordingly at year end

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to: 

Serve written reminders/notices to officials and employees with outstanding cash advances, and require them to immediately submit liquidation reports and/or refund the balance of unused cash advance/s;

Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit to this Office copy of properly accomplished and duly signed Obligation Request for the cash advance of the Municipal Treasurer amounting to ₱50,000.00 per check no. 456438;

Refrain from resorting to procurement through cash withdrawals and cash advances.  All payments should pass through the regular mode of procurement in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 and facilitated by the BAC;

Discontinue the practice of partial liquidation of cash advances; and

Submit a certified copy of the transmittal of liquidation of Intelligence and Confidential funds to the Office of the Chairperson of the Commission on Audit.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to:

Reclassify to the Due to Officers and Employees those  negative cash advances; and 

Transfer from the CIV account to the respective Advances account the withdrawals made by the Municipal Treasurer.

We also recommended the Management to strictly adopt the “no liquidation, no cash advance” policy and implement Section 9.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 which states that:

9.0 Consequences of failure to liquidate

9.1	Failure of an accountable officer to liquidate his outstanding cash advance on or before January 31, 2013 shall constitute cause for the filing of malversation charge under Article 217, failure to liquidate cash advance under Article 218, both of the Revised Penal Code or criminal prosecution under Section 128 of P.D. No. 1445.

9.2	The suspension of salaries of erring accountable officers shall be ordered by the auditor concerned to the proper agency official through the head of the Agency.

Appropriate administrative proceedings shall likewise be instituted.
observations.managementAction: COA Circular 2012-114 is being strictly implemented; RA 9184 is being enforced; Certified copy of the transmittal of liquidation of Intelligence and Confidential funds had been submitted, likewise, cash advance for it has been temporarily stopped; also, the practice of partial liquidation of cash advances had been discontinued; and Due to Officers and Employees accounts had been adjusted accordingly.
observations.reason: Transfer from the CIV account to the respective Advances account the withdrawals made by the Municipal Treasurer needs additional personnel to handle the reconciliation and analysis of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Honorable Municipal Mayor should:

Cause the immediate submission of a complete list of all PPAs in CY 2015 and those that are implemented in CY 2016.

Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004, and

Provide the Audit Team with quarterly updated information as to the status of the publicity of PPAs in order to allow timely validation of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has submitted the requested reports to the audit team

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to fully comply and implement all the audit findings contained in the AAR in CY 2014, and inform this Office on the status of the mentioned findings.
observations.managementAction: To fully comply and implement the audit findings contained in the CY 2014 Annual Audit Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management for the immediate submission of authenticated copies of all the supporting documents for the “Expansion of Pulot Level III Water Supply System” to facilitate in the contract review and technical evaluation and inspection of the said project. 

We further recommended the municipality to refund to the DILG any unutilized balance upon the completion of the project as required under COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.  Otherwise, the municipality should submit an official request to the DILG-Regional Office should they intend to utilize the residual amount, or part of the allocation for which is not used for the purpose of implementing the water supply facilities, in compliance with Section 2 (II)(B)(2g) of the MOA. Disbursements should be supported with complete and proper documentation to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer has submitted the requested documents to the Audit team. As per the bids and awards committee (BAC), the lot used for the project amounting to ₱200,000.00 was part of the 10% Local Government Equity (counterpart) as per MOA and not sourced from the DILG grant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to discontinue the practice of:
Entering into an agreement with private financial institutions wherein the Municipality will act as their collecting agent;
Issuing checks payable to the financial institutions intended for the employees’ loan remittances/repayment; and
Enforcing salary deductions of the employees personal loans.
observations.managementAction: The management is attaching the OBR in all payments made to the different financial institutions with list of employees and their corresponding loan remittances to determine the propriety of the disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the Statement of Financial Performance of the Trust Fund and revised Notes to Financial Statements inclusive of the noted deficiencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had submitted the Statement of Financial Performance of the Trust Fund but there were some corrections found during audit and revisions of those reports had not been accomplished due to time constraint.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the MDRRMO to submit the remaining reports for our reference in audit, otherwise all of the transactions chargeable to the fund will be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: Already submitted to the Audit Team

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal DRRMO and Municipal Accountant to submit a detailed report on the utilization of the said fund for our reference in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal DRRMO and the Municipal Accountant will submit a detailed report on the utilization of the said fund.
observations.reason: The detailed reports required were still not submitted for the year 2016

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to:

Withhold the salaries and other compensation of the persons liable in satisfaction of the disallowance otherwise she will be held liable as per the issued COE which states that:

Payment of salaries or any money due them in violation of this instruction will be disallowed in audit and you will be held liable therefor.

Ensure that officials and employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies are not given clearances unless they have settled the disallowances or have signed an undertaking to deduct the said amount on what is due to them. 

Moreover, the Municipality should take effort on collecting the receivables arising from the ND from the former Municipal Officials who are no longer in government service.
observations.managementAction: Partial settlement through monthly payroll deductions
observations.reason: The amount remained unsettled as of December 31, 2016
